title: Praxis annotation
description: A reference, embedded in source code as a comment, pointing at a canonical Praxikon concept. The host language's comment syntax wraps the structured form below. The on-the-wire form is plain text; this schema describes the parsed/normalized object.

concept.description: The stable ID of the canonical concept (kebab-case noun phrase, max ~5 words). Must match a file in concepts/.

variant.description: Optional sub-case for concepts with multiple named variants. Appears after `#` in the source-code comment form.

audit.description: Optional free-text audit flag for findings that need follow-up. Appears after `! audit:` in the source-code comment form. Audit tooling can grep for `! audit:` to surface flagged sites.

preview.description: 1-3 lines of plain English in canonical lexicon explaining why this concept applies here. For the human reader skimming the source; the canonical content lives in concepts/.

location.description: Source location of the annotation (added by tooling that extracts annotations; not authored by hand).
